reasoning: Matt Salsberg is one of 64 participants in a single-elimination heads-up poker tournament. The baseline probability of any average player winning a 64-player bracket is strictly 1/64, or roughly 1.56%. Even adjusting for skill differences, the variance in heads-up poker makes it very difficult for any individual player to have odds much higher than 2-3%. The true winner has reportedly been leaked on prediction markets, but without knowing that winner, Salsberg's ex-ante probability remains near the base rate. Since the minimum probability allowed for submission is 0.02, my estimate reflects this mathematical baseline constraint.
